deep_dive.title: Stabilising Evaporating Soap Films with Salt
deep_dive.abstract: We investigate the effect of a high concentration (32.5 g.L$^{-1}$) of sodium chloride (NaCl) on TTAB (tetradecyltrimethylammonium bromide) vertical soap films also called foam films, pulled out of a bath under controlled humidity conditions. We observe that the film lifetime increases with relative humidity, both in the presence and absence of salt. At any given humidity, the presence of NaCl systematically enhances film stability. Our film thickness measurements show that the thinning dynamics with or without salt are nearly identical down to 100 nm. Down to that thickness, the effect of evaporation can be rationalised by a constant evaporation rate, which becomes non-negligible compared to the drainage rate at film thicknesses below 400 nm. The main effect of salt is the stabilisation of a Newton black film at a thickness of approximately 5~nm, whereas in the absence of salt, the film ruptures upon reaching a critical thickness of about 10 nm.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: vertical soap film thickness measurements, evaporation, and stabilization with salt

deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 垂直皂膜厚度测量、蒸发及盐稳定化
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This study investigates the stabilization of evaporating vertical soap films by adding NaCl, measuring film thickness and thinning dynamics, which is directly relevant to liquid film thickness measurement techniques.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文实验研究添加氯化钠对垂直皂膜蒸发稳定性的影响。通过测量膜厚和减薄动力学，发现盐的存在显著增强膜稳定性，且减薄行为与无盐时相似直到100nm以下。该研究为液体薄膜厚度测量和垂直降膜蒸发稳定性提供了直接参考。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文实验研究添加氯化钠对垂直皂膜蒸发稳定性的影响。通过测量膜厚和减薄动力学，发现盐的存在显著增强膜稳定性，且减薄行为与无盐时相似直到100nm以下。该研究为液体薄膜厚度测量和垂直降膜蒸发稳定性提供了直接参考。
